title: Make report Op Hours match Total Operator Hours shown in the data-entry form

requirements.text: Ensure the Total Operator Hours value stored on each ProductionEntry in the backend matches the Total Operator Hours value calculated and shown in the Data Entry form, so that the Reports table/exported reports display the exact same operator-hours value for the same entry.

quotes: in report op hours should be equal to total operator hours in form data

acceptanceCriteria: After saving a new production entry, the Reports table column “Op Hours” equals the Data Entry form’s displayed “Total Operator Hours” for that entry (no rounding/threshold differences).
CSV export column “Total Operator Hours” and HTML export column “Operator Hrs/Operator Hrs” match the Data Entry form’s displayed “Total Operator Hours” for the same entry.

The backend no longer stores Total Operator Hours as whole hours only when the Data Entry form would show minutes/seconds for the same entry (i.e., stored hours/minutes/seconds match the form’s computed breakdown).

requirements.text: Align backend operator-hours business logic with the frontend operator-hours utility: (1) use the same duty-time threshold rule as the form (≤ 10 hours uses 10-hour target formula; > 10 hours uses 12-hour target formula), (2) compute Total Operator Hours with fractional precision (minutes/seconds) instead of integer hours-only division, and (3) keep Duty Time lunch-break deduction consistent with the form rule (deduct 30 minutes only when raw duration is < 12 hours).

quotes: in report op hours should be equal to total operator hours in form data

acceptanceCriteria: For any new entry, backend-stored ProductionEntry.totalOperatorHours.{hours,minutes,seconds} matches the frontend calculation performed in frontend/src/utils/operatorHours.ts (calculateTotalOperatorHours + calculateAdjustedDutyTimeSeconds equivalent).
Backend Duty Time adjustment deducts 30 minutes only when raw punch-out minus punch-in duration is strictly less than 12 hours (not when equal to 12 hours).
No backend schema/state-shape migration is introduced unless strictly required; changes are limited to computation logic used when creating entries.

constraints: Respect single-actor backend architecture; keep all Motoko logic in backend/main.mo.
Do not modify files under frontend immutablePaths (including frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts and frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts).
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Recomputing or retroactively backfilling Total Operator Hours for already-saved historical entries (unless explicitly requested later).
Adding new report types, new fields, or changing report filtering UI unrelated to operator-hours consistency.
